PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Nov-06-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Katelyn Wheeler, Esq., counsel for and with Colleen Mauer, Petitioner and Special Administrator (appearing separately by video conference).

The Court notes the filing of a pleading by Ms. Wheeler but, due to its belated receipt, has not been reviewed by the Court. Nonetheless, the Court advises Ms. Wheeler that on the record now before the Court, it would require more competent evidence to support the Petitioner's efforts to locate the decedent's mother and verified pleadings to support Ms. Wheeler's reporting on interactions with the decedent's father and his ostensibly obstreperous behavior. Ms. Wheeler reports that the belatedly filed pleadings would address the Court's concerns.

The Court next addresses the Examiner's contention that the facts and circumstances of this case require the filing of a petition by the Petitioner herein as an Administrator with Will annexed as opposed to an executor of a holographic Will as the subject Will does not specifically identify an executor. Ms. Wheeler refers the Court to Probate Code Secs. 8421 and 6111.5 to support her contention that a Will need not specifically identify an executor and that extrinsic evidence may be considered in aiding and clarifying the purpose and intent of a Will and the administration of the same. By way of informal tentative ruling, the Court advises counsel that it remains of the opinion that the Petition should be amended to petition for appointment as Administrator with Will annexed. Upon the Court's inquiry, Ms. Wheeler advises that the Petitioner did not seek to administer the estate by affidavit due to the adversarial posture of the decedent's father and the fact he had already taken possession of some of the decedent's personal property.

Court and counsel further discuss the difficulties the Petitioner has encountered in attempting to provide notice of the proceedings. The Court advises it would waive publication of the amended petition should Ms. Wheeler elect that route. After further discussion, Court recesses for 10 minutes.

After recess, the Court notes review of Petitioner's belated pleadings and after further consideration of Probate Code Secs. 8421 and 6111.5, finds that the petition as filed and the notice, provided and attempted, are sufficient for the Court's consideration. The Court admits the undated holographic Will of Kamran Hatami, filed October 8, 2020, into probate. The Court will take the Petition under submission subject to the filing of a verified pleading by Ms. Mauer affirming the essential facts stated in earlier filed declaration. Subject to that filing, the Court will set hearing for November 3, 2021 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204 for status of closing the estate. A Petition for final distribution or for additional time to administer the estate must be filed one week prior to that hearing date.
After inquiry by the Court and at Ms. Wheeler's request, the Court extends the Letters of Special Administration for Ms. Mauer to December 4, 2020.

Judge: Hon. Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Deputy: K. Kabasares; Reporter: Not Reported. (D204)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF SPECIAL ADMINISTRATION WITH GENERAL POWERS
